title: Top-K Frequent Elements
description:
Given an integer array, return the k most frequent elements in any order. Use a counter to compute frequencies, then maintain a size-k min-heap keyed by frequency. This teaches combining hash maps with priority queues to keep the best k items while streaming.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ len(nums) ≤ 10^5
- k ≥ 1
explanation:
Intuition
Counting compresses duplicates. A min-heap of size k retains the top k frequencies. Replace the root when a higher frequency appears.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Tally counts with a hash map
2. Main Algorithm: Iterate (num, freq) and push into a min-heap; pop when size exceeds k
3. Return Result: Return the heap contents

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n log k)
- Space Complexity: O(n)

Key Insights
- Map+heap pattern is common for streaming top-k
- Heap stores at most k items

Common Pitfalls
- Building a max-heap is unnecessary in Python
- Forgetting to return values, not (freq, val) pairs
hints: Count with a hash map first
Use a size-k min-heap keyed by frequency
Extract the values from the heap at the end
